commentary: 0.1 Siraj to Nissanka, no runTouch back of length outside off, cut with the swing to cover point
0.2 Siraj to Nissanka, FOUR runsToo full and wide, Nissanka thrashes it through cover. Diagonal-bat drive, hit with ball next to his back leg and with the swing

0.3 Siraj to Nissanka, FOUR runsTakes a step down and goes over the bowler. Got the ball beside him and then drove through the line. Ball just in front of him. Maintained full control even after connecting
0.4 Siraj to Nissanka,  1 runLength outside off, guided to deep third
0.5 Siraj to Rahul,  1 runSwings away. Guided with the swing to deep-backward point. Didn't move his feet, opened the face and patted the length ball with a vertical bat

0.6 Siraj to Nissanka, FOUR runsMan is Nissanka in touch! This is an authoritative straight drive. Small stride forward, got the hands down quickly and continued the swing till around rib-height. Held his shape for the snappers
1.1 Rabada to Rahul, no runStopped at midwicket. Flicks the 141ks full ball off the front foot
1.2 Rabada to Rahul, no runLength on top of middle and leg, same pace, defended with a closing bat-face towards midwicket
1.3 Rabada to Rahul, FOUR runsSmacked over wide long-on. Rahul's ready for the fullness in length. Takes a small stride forward and swings the bat from third through mid-on. Full follow-through
1.4 Rabada to Rahul,  1 runCut to deep point. Wide on a length, Rahul getting low and middling it
1.5 Rabada to Nissanka, no runLength outside off, looks to punch through cover with a mostly horizontal bat. Under-edge, on the bounce to the keeper

1.5 Rabada to Nissanka, 1 wideTries first up and fails. Pre-meditated scoop aimed fine, got his front leg outside off and showed all three stumps. Was on one knee, the back of length ball went down leg
1.6 Rabada to Nissanka, no runBeaten on the cut. 145ks outswinger hits a hard length and goes away outside off. Nissanka, with planted feet, tried to go through point
2.1 Siraj to Rahul,  2 runsPitched up at the stumps, driven over the bowler. Lands at the edge of the outfield, long-off runs left to stop it
2.2 Siraj to Rahul, no runSlower one goes under the bat. Oh no, that's 134ks. Back of length ball died down after hitting the pitch, Rahul went for the pull
2.3 Siraj to Rahul, no runFollowed as he made room. Got a length ball that he flicked to midwicket
2.4 Siraj to Rahul, FOUR runsWide again and that flies. It's swinging off the fuller side of length, but in Rahul's reach. He opens the face and lifts it over mid-off
2.5 Siraj to Rahul, no runBeaten. Back of lengther well outside off but the bounce is a touch low. Rahul takes a step across and looks to cut hard over cover.
2.6 Siraj to Rahul, no runLength on middle and leg, punched to mid-on
2.6 Siraj to Rahul, 1 wideDown leg, Buttler takes it low with one hand. Length ball goes down with the angle, past the leg glance
3.1 Rabada to Nissanka,  1 runNot too short, pulled through square leg

3.2 Rabada to Rahul,  1 runFull toss slips a touch high, almost creates a wicket. Rahul wanted to lift it behind square. He wasn't fully upright and the ball was rearing at him, so he removed his bottom hand and lifted it with a straight bat. Ball landed safely behind square with fielders converging
3.3 Rabada to Nissanka,  1 runHelped off the back foot to deep fine leg
3.4 Rabada to Rahul,  SIX runsPicked up over the DC dugout at square leg. Too full and on the pads, Rahul bends his back leg a touch, closes the face as he swings from back to front
3.5 Rabada to Rahul, no runDeserved more than a dot. 147ks hard-length ball was flat-batted, Rahul forced momentum against the ball and timed it well. Found mid-off
3.6 Rabada to Rahul, no runRabada's missing a few yorkers, bowls a shin-high full toss that's driven to cover
4.1 Rashid Khan to Nissanka, no runGoogly snakes back off a length towards leg stump, cut to cover point. 87ks
4.2 Rashid Khan to Nissanka,  1 run96ks length ball on leg, swept on the rise to deep midwicket
4.3 Rashid Khan to Rahul,  1 runDriven into a length at 90ks, on the pads, whipped off the back foot to long-on. CLeared the front leg too
4.4 Rashid Khan to Nissanka,  1 runInto the pitch at 89ks, on middle but Nissanka happy to play a cross-batted pull through midwicket

4.5 Rashid Khan to Rahul, no runWarne-like spin. Angles in and lands towards off before snaking away fiercely. Rahul didn't chase it, kept the bat on pad while shaping to defend to cover. Happily beaten. Rashid smiles
4.6 Rashid Khan to Rahul,  1 runLength on off, punched to long-off

5.1 Ashok Sharma to Rahul, FOUR runsEven Phillips can't chase that. Bottom edge went very high and going away towards the point boundary. Phillips went out from the infield and dove forward, couldn't get fingertips on it. 143ks hard-length ball on off, Rahul made room and carved it
5.2 Ashok Sharma to Rahul,  1 runLow full toss on leg, flicked to deep square leg. 147ks
5.3 Ashok Sharma to Nissanka, FOUR runsSlower one picked. Nissanka also makes room, has to fetch as the full ball was outside off. Managed to get it with a side-swipe over extra cover

5.4 Ashok Sharma to Nissanka,  SIX runsComes in at pace, same for going away. 147ks but a bit too full, Nissanka's beside the ball and drives through the line. High back-lift, bat-path was almost from first slip through mid-on but the face opened on impact and it went straight
5.5 Ashok Sharma to Nissanka, FOUR runsMet with again. 148ks length ball just outside off, clobbered across the line with a clean swing. Over midwicket
5.6 Ashok Sharma to Nissanka, FOUR runsSlower one... still guided wide of short third. That's a use-the-pace shot. Length outside off, Nissanka taps it on the head. Timeout
6.1 Prasidh to Rahul,  1 runLength around off, flicked to midwicket
6.2 Prasidh to Nissanka, no runLength on middle and leg, 137ks, Nissanka looks to flick through midwicket. Leading edge goes into the the pitch in front
6.3 Prasidh to Nissanka,  2 runsPitched up on middle at 142ks, flicked through backward square leg
6.4 Prasidh to Nissanka, no runLength down leg, beats the leg glance and goes off the pad to the keeper. 137ks
6.5 Prasidh to Nissanka,  1 runLength outside off, mix of a cut and a back-foot drive through cover. 141ks
6.6 Prasidh to Rahul, FOUR runsWidth offered and that's slapped over extra cover. GT lucky that didn't land over the ropes. Hard length allows Rahul to flay it with a flat bat
7.1 Rashid Khan to Nissanka,  1 runLength just outside off, 88ks, cut to deep point
7.2 Rashid Khan to Rahul,  1 runPitched up on off, defended towards cover.
7.3 Rashid Khan to Nissanka,  1 runPitched up on leg, flicked to long-on
7.4 Rashid Khan to Rahul, no runShaped for the cut but that kept coming in, Rahul finished almost playing a flick to the 89ks length ball. Hits high on the bat and lands nearby
7.5 Rashid Khan to Rahul,  1 runLooped at 88ks, full too, driven just over the infield and to long-on
7.6 Rashid Khan to Nissanka,  1 runLength ball lands on leg and goes in, flicked through midwicket

8.1 Prasidh to Nissanka, OUTPrasidh continues his wicket-taking ways. Bowls hard length just outside off with a slightly scrambled seam, it's a bit too close and short for the flat-bat slash across the line. Mid-off takes a low catch. Nissanka tosses his bat forward and spins it as GT celebratePathu
8.2 Prasidh to Rana,  1 runLooks to crush it across the line, the fullish ball goes off the inside-half of the bat to deep square leg
8.3 Prasidh to Rahul, FOUR runsDeep square leg is close but has no chance. One bounce four to his left. Was a shortish ball around rib-height, pace-on, Rahul swivelled and pulled it flat
8.4 Prasidh to Rahul,  SIX runsWaits for and muscles the slower one. 100ks offcutter is on the fuller side just outside off, Rahul stays leg side of the ball and swings straight. 74-metres six

8.5 Prasidh to Rahul,  SIX runsPace-on and an even better shot! Inside out over wide long-off. Overpitched outside of, Rahul doesn't move his feet much, knows he's close to the ball and drives with a slightly open face

8.6 Prasidh to Rahul, FOUR runsRahul takes on the best bowler in the opposition and gets to fifty. Removes his gloves and rocks on with a celebration. This had width and the shortness, carved through backward point Previous NextPhotos
9.1 Rashid Khan to Rana, no runFull on leg, 95ks, flicked to short fine leg

9.2 Rashid Khan to Rana, no runReverse sweep missed, big lbw appeal. Given! DC review. Length ball went over the bat and looked to have hit him just over the right glove. Ultra-Edge shows a spike as the ball's just over the bat. So Rana survives
9.3 Rashid Khan to Rana, no runLength on leg, beats the flick and hits the pad
9.4 Rashid Khan to Rana, FOUR runsShort and it's pulled over midwicket. Spun in towards off, Rana swivelled and powered it flat. Nice jump to finish

9.5 Rashid Khan to Rana, OUTHigh ball... Sudharsan runs in takes it beautifully! Running in from long-off, took the catch as he went down to slide, then tumbled and turned to get up in style. Was a 95ks length ball outside off that Rana swiped across the line. Top edge. Small innings, eventful but n

9.6 Rashid Khan to Sameer Rizvi, OUTPOTM... not today. Ripping googly from Rashid. Lands just outside off and spins in sharply, Rizvi covered the initial line as he went for the punch through mid-off. Beaten on the inside edge, off stump dinkedSameer Rizvi b Rashid Khan 0 (1b 0x4 0x6 1m) SR: 0 Previ

10.1 Sundar to Rahul,  SIX runsbut nothing matters for Rahul!! He sends the ball up a few hundred metres, and lands it beyond the long-on boundary. Down the track, and converts this into a length ball for a free swing of the arms
10.2 Sundar to Rahul,  1 runshort of a length, and just outside off. Swatted to long-off
10.3 Sundar to Miller,  1 runfull and flat, and just outside off. Driven to square leg off the inside half of the bat
10.4 Sundar to Rahul,  1 runshorter side of a length on the stumps, and turning in. Punched to long-on
10.5 Sundar to Miller,  2 runson a length on leg, and he goes back to clip to deep-backward square leg for two

10.6 Sundar to Miller, no runthis skids through and strikes Miller, but the lbw appeal for Washy is turned down. On a length on middle, and that seemed to be heading down with the angle as Miller looked to clip but missed
11.1 Ashok Sharma to Rahul,  1 runshort in length, and just outside off. Pulled powerfully to long-on
11.2 Ashok Sharma to Miller, no runextra bounce for Ashok. He bowls this slower, and goes for a bouncer wide-ish of off. Miller looks to pull but is beaten

11.3 Ashok Sharma to Miller,  1 runbottom edge, and they push for the single. There was a throw at the bowler's end even as Miller dived. The throw was wide, though. Miller went for the pull off this short-of-a-length ball shaping in, but played a bit early
11.4 Ashok Sharma to Rahul,  1 runchange of pace. Short of a length around off at 103kph, and nudged to midwicket
11.5 Ashok Sharma to Miller,  1 runshort of a length, and just outside off. Pace on this time, and punched to mid-of for a hurried single

11.5 Ashok Sharma to Miller, 1 widevery wide. In fact, the umpire's wondering if it's even a no-ball for landing outside the pitch. Nope, it's called a wide. Ashok went for the slower ball on a short of a length, and around 15th stump. Miller let it go
11.6 Ashok Sharma to Rahul,  1 runkeeps mixing it up. Another slower ball at 110kph. This is on a length, and around sixth stump. Swatted back towards extra cover
12.1 Prasidh to Rahul,  1 runa full toss on off, and whipped to deep-backward square leg

12.2 Prasidh to Miller, FOUR runsfour to deep midwicket. The pull wasn't timed all that convincingly, but it still came off. In fact, he was wringing his left hand after pulling it away. Short ball just outside off, and he went after it
12.3 Prasidh to Miller, no runon the bounce to short fine leg. He swivels to pull another short ball, but doesn't find the gap this time
12.4 Prasidh to Miller,  2 runsmuch fuller and slower. Miller flicks it late to wide long-on, and picks two
12.5 Prasidh to Miller,  1 runnearly a yorker, and on middle. Miller clips to deep midwicket this time

12.6 Prasidh to Rahul, FOUR runsand he gets it. Rahul goes over extra cover. He stands deep in the crease, and lofts this inside out. Prasidh bowls a full delivery outside off, and Rahul forcefully goes after the ball Previous NextPhotos
13.1 Rashid Khan to Axar Patel,  1 runfull and tossed up on middle. Driven to long-on
13.2 Rashid Khan to Rahul,  1 runon a few bounces to Washy at fine leg. Rahul sweeps this full ball on middle and off by going down
13.3 Rashid Khan to Axar Patel,  1 runon a length on off, and punched to cover point
13.4 Rashid Khan to Rahul, no runquite full and straight, and pushed back to Rashid
13.5 Rashid Khan to Rahul,  1 runagain bowls very full, and this is quicker too. Flicked from middle to deep midwicket

13.6 Rashid Khan to Axar Patel, OUTPhillips does what he does best - takes a good catch!! Rashid went across the stumps, and bowled from around the wicket. It pitched on a full length on off, and Rashid bowled the wrong 'un. Axar went lofting by opening the face of his bat, but ended up slicing it f

14.1 Rabada to Rahul, FOUR runsbang!! Four over the bowler's head. The length is full enough for him to simply hit through the line. He targets this ball on off, and sends it sailing back down the ground
14.1 Rabada to Rahul, 1 widewide ball. Rabada angles it in, and lands it full and outside leg. Rahul looked to flick but missed
14.2 Rabada to Rahul,  1 runshort ball a little wide off, and at a lack of pace. Rahul rolls his wrists to pull to deep midwicket
14.3 Rabada to Stubbs,  1 runon a length on off, and punched to long-on

14.4 Rabada to Rahul,  1 runIt's Phillips at deep midwicket, and he flies to his right to take it on the bounce. Rahul pulls a bouncer from outside off, and his top hand comes off the bat. Just a single in the end
14.5 Rabada to Stubbs, no runshort ball just outside off, and Stubbs looks to pull but is beaten by the pace and movement back in
14.6 Rabada to Stubbs, no runStubbs wants a single, but lets it be. Rabada bowls short on middle, and hits Stubbs on leg as the latter opens his stance up to nudge
15.1 Ashok Sharma to Rahul, FOUR runsfour first ball after the break. Ashok bowls from wide of the crease, and short on middle. Rahul beats short fine leg to his left after pulling it away
15.2 Ashok Sharma to Rahul,  1 runback of a length, and just outside off. Back to pace on at 143kph. Punched to extra cover
15.2 Ashok Sharma to Rahul, 1 widegoes full, but wide of off. The length is very full, but Rahul lets it go

15.3 Ashok Sharma to Stubbs,  5 runswell bowled. Then there's a direct hit. Then there's an overthrow... for four. So it's five off the ball. Wow, Ashok fired a yorker on off, only for it to cost five. Stubbs jammed it down towards mid-off, from where Gill threw at the striker's end. It hit the stum

15.4 Ashok Sharma to Rahul, FOUR runsSai at long-on turns into Phillips, but neither can he catch nor can he save four. Length ball at a lack of pace outside off, and Rahul flings at it. Sai dives forward, and the ball falls well short of him, before popping up off his hands for four

15.5 Ashok Sharma to Rahul,  1 runhigh full toss on middle, and punched to long-on. It's not given no-ball for height, so Rahul reviews. Oh, the first replay shows Rahul played it from below the waist, but we'll see. Yup, he has played it well below his waist height. So it's a fair delivery
15.6 Ashok Sharma to Stubbs, no runbeaten by the change of pace. Ashok goes full at 105kph, and around sixth stump. Stubbs has a punch but misses

16.1 Siraj to Rahul,  1 rungood take by Washy. He was at deep backward point, and took the ball on the bounce even as it spun away from him. Rahul sliced a low full toss outside off, and didn't find the gap
16.2 Siraj to Stubbs,  1 runRashid saves three runs. He's at mid-off, and throws himself to his right to get a hand. Stubbs drilled this full ball outside off, but only got one
16.3 Siraj to Rahul, no runwow, Siraj bowls a bouncer and Rahul looks for the reverse swat but misses. The ball pitches wide-ish of off, and away from Rahul's range

16.4 Siraj to Rahul, OUTdirect hit, and Stubbs is gone!! Sai was at mid-on, and had a lot of time to aim at the bowler's end as Stubbs had taken off. But Stubbs was sent back after running halfway down, and even a dive couldn't save him. Siraj bowled another bouncer at Rahul, who swatted at it from

16.5 Siraj to Rahul, FOUR runsthere's no long-on, and Rahul spots that. One bounce, and four. But Phillips from deep midwicket gives it his all while running to his left. A full delivery around off, and Rahul lofts it over mid-on
16.5 Siraj to Rahul, 1 wideone more. Yorker length as the ball swings away to land wide of off. Rahul lets it go
16.5 Siraj to Rahul, 1 widewide delivery. Rahul leaves it alone, as it is very full and wide of off

16.6 Siraj to Rahul, OUTgot him!! Rahul falls for 92. This could be the game for GT. Rahul chases a wide delivery, and falls. A cross-seam delivery from Siraj, who bowls on a yorker length and wide outside off. Rahul had left the previous wide balls outside off this over, but not this time. He has a
17.1 Rabada to Miller,  1 runshort ball on middle, and shapes away. Pulled to deep midwicket
17.2 Rabada to Nigam,  2 runslands between point and deep point running towards each other. A short-of-a-length ball outside off, and Nigam sliced it aerially
17.3 Rabada to Nigam, no rungets across the stumps, and looks to scoop, but misses. Rabada saw the batter moving, and bowled it short and wide of off. The ball went over Nigam in the end
17.4 Rabada to Nigam,  1 runthis time Nigam backs away. Short ball outside off, and swatted to long-on
17.5 Rabada to Miller, FOUR runscrashed over extra cover. Short delivery finishing on off, and he waits before tennis-batting the ball over the in-field
17.6 Rabada to Miller,  1 runshort again, and pulled from middle to fine leg. There's a cry from Miller, possibly on hitting the ball straight to the fielder
18.1 Siraj to Miller,  SIX runspumped over deep midwicket!! Short ball pitching on leg, and shaping away. Miller picks it up from wide-ish of off, and rams the pull for six
18.1 Siraj to Miller, 1 widewide ball. Siraj angles it across, and goes on a yorker length wide of off. Left alone by Miller

18.2 Siraj to Miller, FOUR runssix more?! I think it landed bang on the boundary cushion. But the third umpire will confirm. Siraj went full this time, but the line was wide of off. Miller launched the ball forcefully, and went over extra cover. We're still waiting for confirmation regarding it was

18.3 Siraj to Miller,  SIX runsMiller doesn't even need to look. He still gets six. His eyes are facing the ground, even as the ball has found its way into the sky. Full delivery around middle, and thumped back over long-on for six more. Who would believe Miller had hurt his hand while both fielding
18.4 Siraj to Miller,  1 runshort ball again. This pitches outside leg, and Miller pulls after the ball finishes on middle. Single to deep midwicket

18.5 Siraj to Nigam, FOUR runsfour for Nigam!! All Buttler can do is watch the ball sail over his head. Bouncer from Siraj, and this pitches outside off but ends on middle. There's a man at deep third, but the late upper cut is late enough for Nigam to get four
18.6 Siraj to Nigam,  1 runNigam backs away, and Siraj follows. It ends up being a high-ish full toss way outside leg. Nigam whips to fine leg, even as I thought they wouldn't run

19.1 Prasidh to Nigam, FOUR runsover mid-off. Why do you need a Miller when a Nigam is on song?! He's deep in the crease, and slices this length ball on the stumps. There's no long-off, and Nigam has all the room to swing his arms

19.2 Prasidh to Nigam, OUTtaken by Gill!! More importantly for GT, Miller will remain off strike. More drama. A short ball from Prasidh, who bowls a cutter outside off. The ball shapes back in, and Nigam decides to swat it back after making room. But he can only send it up for Gill to take it after
19.3 Prasidh to Kuldeep,  1 runa loud cheer from the crowd as Kuldeep takes the single. Short delivery just outside off, and Kuldeep guides it to deep third

19.4 Prasidh to Miller,  SIX runsthis lands out of the ground!! A few hundred miles over long-off. Gill looks away. We'll need a new ball. Miller gets a length delivery outside off, and he liberally lofts it through the line. He got the pace and the length to go for it.
19.5 Prasidh to Miller, no runohhh, he sends Kuldeep back. Dot ball for GT. Wow, Miller will need two off one ball. Short ball, and he pulled from middle to deep-backward square leg

19.6 Prasidh to Miller, OUTHave GT have won by one run?! This is an amazing finish. The umpire has given Kuldeep run-out, but hang on... this could be wide for height. Plenty is happening even as GT look confident. The players are lurking on the field, waiting for the final decision. Ohh, the ball p
